label: Collecting and Identifying Lake Superior Agates

comments.text: A few agates I've found at Lake Superior in recent years.
I’m doing way too well in my agate game this year at work. Two days in and 16 really decent Lake Superior agates already

A very brief thread about #minerals and colour. Minerals come in every colour of the rainbow. Here's what's in my header image 🌈💎 #science 🧪 Crocoite - Tasmania, Australia
Wulfenite - Arizona, USA
Sulphur - Sicily, Italy
Malachite - Russia
Azurite - Arizona, USA
Amethyst - Lake Superior, Canada
I’ve found some absolutely solid Lake Superior agates on job sites this year
I’ve found 31 more Lake Superior agates since yesterday, and this one from today is a top 3 find in 30 years for me.
Had an amazing couple hours hunting agates today. A gorgeous sunny day with Lake Superior splashing on my face and showing me some amazing rocks!
Finally getting last week's Lake Superior Agates cleaned up

None of my lake Superior agates are actually from Lake Superior they're from random places along the Mississippi River, mostly, and these are a couple of my favorites that I found just now, looking for stones to make letters out of

How Agates Form, Why Minnesota Lakes Have So Many, & Where To Look For Them. I was always excited as a kid to go find agates at the lake (typically Lake Superior), but I didn’t know a) how they formed, or b) that you couldn’t find them just anywhere. [thekidshouldseethis.com]

Watercolor painting of agates inspired by the beautiful rocks of Lake Superior (my favorite place on earth). ⛵ #watercolor #watercolorart #watercolorartist #agates #LakeSuperior #MN #fineart #painting #artist #art

Here is a pic of Lake Superior agates. Everything is so awful right now, I feel desperate for beauty. These formed around 1.5 billion years ago, then they were tumbled in the Lake after the glaciers retreated. Not my collection, but I wish it was!
Treasures from our camping trip to Lake Superior. Beautiful weather, great camping spot, and gorgeous rocks. A few agates. Happy!!

Eating lunch, and then fuck it, I guess I'm going to try going at some rocks with my Dremel. A few that I want to tumble kind of need to be cleaned up to tumble right, and I've got a few whole, small Lake Superior agates I'd like to cut after practicing on something that isn't super important to me.
Here are more Lake Superior agates, partially polished on my King Cab.
Hi there. I live in Michigan and have a “camp” in the Upper Peninsula on Lake Superior. I hunt for agates (one of which is pictured below). Trying this site out for the first time.
Anyone else do some Rockhounding for Agates? Found in the U.P. On Lake Superior. #agate #LSA #rockhounding #Gemstones
Leaving Lake Superior agates as a tribute to Alex Pretti is a particularly thoughtful touch. www.dnr.state.mn.us/education/ge...

I look for necklaces with cool chains at thrift stores for jewelry projects. Yesterday I suddenly remembered I had tiny greenstones and Lake Superior agates my Dad and I found on one of rock collecting trips. I added a UP charm that had been waiting for the right project.
Love opals. They are my favorite stone but unfortunately not found where I live. Lots of Lake Superior agates tho!
Looking for Michigan Rockhounds; Great Lakes Rockhounds. The best people 👆💛 #rocks #minerals #rockhounds #agates #fossils
I hunt for, and make things with Lake Superior agates and other stones I find.

#rockhound
#rochpolidhing Here is the range for Lake Superior Agates. They were moved sough by glaciers and rivers.
So do I, thanks to my dad. 😆 Lake Superior Agates too.

If my husband were well enough for me to travel, I would make a trip up one side of Lake Michigan and then over to the beach on the other side and perhaps one or two on the lower end of Superior. Agates are my dearest love for making jewelry.
More agates from the past 2 years. All found by me, all at Lake Superior in the Eastern UP.

A year of rockhounding! :-) so many cool finds this year, most of which is not sorted because. I picked up a lot of rocks, to be frank. But found some great lake superior agates this year, along with some fossils! Can't wait to see what I find in the next year.

My sweetest uncle is an agate guy. He finds them on the shore of Lake Superior and shines them up real nice. When he sees little kids looking for agates, he’ll say, oh I think there’s a bunch over there (indicating a spot he just loaded up for them)
Tumbling Lake Superior rocks, these are going to stage 2! Each stage can be up to two weeks, my moss Agates took 6 months!
That’s amazing never seen one like that always heard Lake Superior agates were the best they really are looking at this
newly single and ready to..... spend hours rockhounding agates from local riverbeds. just a few of my recent finds, including a 2.5oz lake superior agate (largest I've found yet!)

Chlorastrolite, aka Isle Royale Greenstone, is a precious gem found only in this world on Isle Royale. It is Michigan's official State Gem. This beauty was laying in the water on the shoreline of Lake Superior!
Lake Superior Agates began to be formed around 1.5 billion years ago, from volcanic activity. The majority of the agates were pushed west and south by glaciers, into Wisconsin, Minnesota, and Iowa.
I just recently realized Lake Superior Agates are a thing, too.
Thank you. I feel blessed every time I get to go to Lake Superior and hunt these beauties!
I love what you do; just followed you. I find hunting agates at Lake Superior is very good for me. The sound of the waves, patterns on the rocks, and miles of coastline are so soothing!

New Agates! Lake Superior Agates, each with it's own style!
love to my lapidary friends
cannot even imagine that level of humiliation & would know right then that it’s best to fill my pockets with agates & walk into lake superior
I want to go hunting near Lake Superior for those incredible agates!

awesome a fellow rock licker. Love the great lakes area, lots of great hounding areas. Pudding atones, agates, amethyst and tons of other stuff. I'm originally from the ottawa Valley area but now on the prairies.
I got this side table for $30 at Great Lakes Antiques Boutique in Grayslake, IL! I found the globe there, too. It’s quickly becoming my one of my fav places #antiques #thrifting #secondhand
Dang, I have been to both Idaho and Lake Superior & had zero luck with agates! Impressive!
some lil hand painted studio ghibli kodamas that glow in the dark, with lake superior agates and dried flowers 🌱💚 pendants~ $50

Ooh, I’ve seen some Lake Superior agates, but never had an opportunity to hunt for them 😍 and we’ve all done it lol. (But as a geology degree-holder, I am contractually obligated to point out that rocks are composed of many elements and minerals which are historically bad for the human body. 😅)
I licked so many rocks this summer looking for agates on Lake Superior, haha, this is great.
One of the best picking days of the spring Minnesota Lake Superior Agates. #mn #agate #lakesuperioragate
These are so beautiful! I hunt Kentucky and Lake Superior Agates in the USA and am amazed at the beauty in these agates around the world! Thank you for sharing!

That's how I got the majority of my collection, up until I was an adult. When my dad was getting out if the rock cutting/polishing hobby, I got all his stones- whole bucket of Lake Superior Agates, Petosky stones, Amethyst...

That's gorgeous!
I can't wait to go look for agates around Lake Superior!
May I ask what kind of saw you are using? I have a ton of Lake Superior agates I'd like to cut and/or polish some day. Yours came out really nice!
May I introduce you to the coolest rocks around: Lake Superior Agates? (Also cool: yooperlite, Isle Royal Greenstone, and petosky stones) (Also also cool: fossils, petrified wood, and mammoth teeth)

That's amazing! Lake Superior agates have such beautiful fortification banding from those ancient volcanic flows. The glacial transport really tumbled them into those perfect rounded shapes. Love seeing the classic red iron oxide banding - geological art at its finest! 🔥
We call them Lake Superior agates. They were eroded out of 1.1 billion year-old basalt formations by glaciers during the ice ages of the past 2 million years. Here's what they look like:
Nice painted LSA. #Agates #CoolRocks #Camping #Outdoors #LakeSuperior #UpperPeninsulaMichigan

As a life long learner, I appreciate learning new cool shit. This year, I learned a bit about geology. How amygdaloids of silicate form in bubble pockets in basalt over a billion years. Then 10k yrs ago glaciers bust them open and scatter them across the Great Lakes region. That's how we get agates.
Who loves agates? Me!! Lake Superior Agate at its finest! Amethyst center!! 🤓❤️💥💥👀
Beautiful agate in the middle. Ive got a lot of Lake Superior agates.

I think where you are talking about, is where all the LSAs come from! Lake Superior Agates! I want to go up there and rock hound it. Bad pic here, but I have tons that I found over the years. It's a serious obsession. But we are weeks away from morel season, so that will be the first hunt.

I'm from MN, so our lake superior agates are more red and orange. But yours, in addition to being blues and greys has a quasi-opalescent sheen, so I thought maybe there were metallic particulates or something. Wicked cool. Yes, I'm trying to bring wicked back, sans evil, sans boston.
I hunt agates, mostly at Lake Superior. I'm not a geologist, but I love the variety of stones I find; jasper, quartz, rhyolite, unakite, iron, amythist, and prehnite besides agate.
My favorite rocks are LSAs Lake Superior Agates, for their redness. Jasper is a close second!!! Same intense red, and some of the same lines and crystals you see in agates! These are lovely!!!

I feel this way when I find lake superior agates. Look around, what else can you find and hold in your hand that is between 1 and 2 billion years old. Just incredible. Makes me feel connected to it all.

I would love to see a picture of it because sections of all of these have orange in them. Not much, not the prominent color, but it's there. If it formed in limestone, it's cold water agate. Lake Superior agates form in silica rich matrix.
MN (as I'm sure you know) is great for rockhounding too! I've got a nice collection of Lake Superior agates from all over the state!
UP and EUP agates are also Lake Superior agates, but generally with distinctive characteristics of their localities.
All those Lake Superior agates are otherworldly beautiful! Thanks for exciting (to us rock lovers) pics!
Lake Superior agates, btw—Minnesota’s official state gemstone. Nice touch.
Wowzers. I never find any Lake Superior agates. Not because I don't try though.
if i ever got a notif saying this, i would simply fill my pockets with agates & walk into lake superior
Wow! So lucky. I have to drive 6 hours to Lake Superior to find agates.
Upstate Michigan has some amazing finds! Hope you come across some Lake Superior agates or maybe some Petoskey stones. The glacial deposits up there can really surprise you. Enjoy the hunt! 🔍

The Lake Superior agates up there are incredible! That oolitic jasper with the spherical structures can show amazing patterns when polished. The iron formations in that area create some really unique color combinations too.
-jordan.bsky.social here is a thread of a few of my Lake Superior agates. Just scroll through the comments to see more. I'd love to hear what you think.
I'm passing by Lake Superior tomorrow and one of my plans is to stop and look for agates, at least for a few minutes! We'll be literally driving right past a great beach.

A few of the many variations on agate. I live in the realm of Lake Superior Agates.
Not many blues in LSAs, mostly reddish.
I go to Lake Superior beaches to relax and rockhound for agates and/or rocks that strike a fancy
I was always pleased to find agates washed up along the rocky beaches dotting the Lake Superior shoreline.
I used to like hunting for agates on Lake Superior. Thanks for sharing the pic.
She's my spirit animal! I hunt for agates at Lake Superior, and make things out of the rocks I find.

A Yooper? Then you may know something about "sisu", which I learned about only through a couple of children's books written by Jennifer Holm, set just northwest of this area, when it was being settled. The only thing I've done in the U.P. was to collect some agates along the Lake Superior shore.
Agates?? I love rockhounding too!! Mostly agates. We have Lake Superior Agates here, more red because of the iron.
This reminds me of my childhood. I collected lots of agates around Lake Superior and other lakes. Wish I knew where they were now. My guess is that my mom threw them in the garden! 😂
Top Lt: chert with quartz in the crack, top Rt: Lake Superior agates, bottom Lt: mica with tiny garnets, bottom Rt: mica in gneiss.

What a fantastic way to celebrate Lake Superior agates! Those fortification patterns and red-orange banding from iron minerals are absolutely iconic. The artistic challenge will be capturing that translucent depth illusion on a flat license plate design.
